scope: Tier-3 platform + API excellence, cycle 1 of 3 (per project_propmanager_path_forward.md). Phases 22 (perf+load), 23 (a11y), 24 (i18n+swahili) closed Tier 2; Phase 25 [API-SURFACE] takes the API from 'functional internal contract' to 'world-class published contract'. The codebase today exposes a 57-route Sanctum-authenticated API (routes/api.php) across two versions (v1 broad + v2 single-endpoint), 9 Resource classes, 5 Api FormRequests, and 19 Api controllers — but it has ZERO machine-readable spec, NO documented versioning policy, NO Sunset/Deprecation header convention, NO API-key-management UI (Sanctum PATs exist server-side via createToken but landlords cannot self-serve), NO standardized rate-limit headers (Laravel throttles via 429 but no X-RateLimit-* envelope), NO webhook-subscription self-serve UI (the Mpesa/IntaSend/bank/WhatsApp webhooks are inbound from providers — landlords cannot register OUTbound webhooks for their own integrations), and NO standardized error response schema. A third-party integrator today reads routes/api.php + tries calls — the contract is the source code, not a published artifact. Phase 25 makes the API a first-class platform: spec generated and served, versioning policy documented, deprecation contract enforced via headers, landlord-facing API-key + webhook self-serve, X-RateLimit envelope standardized, error responses standardized to a single shape, and CI watchdogs that fail the build if routes drift from the spec. 18 findings across 7 categories: API-DOC (3 — OpenAPI spec source of truth, served Swagger/Redoc UI, generated changelog), API-VERSION (3 — versioning policy doc, Sunset+Deprecation headers, consumer-facing deprecation log), API-AUTH (4 — API-key management UI, last-used tracking, audit-log entries, expiration policy), API-RATELIMIT (3 — X-RateLimit headers, 429 problem+json body, per-token override policy), API-WEBHOOK (3 — webhook subscription self-serve, delivery log + retry UI, event catalog), API-ERROR (1 — standardized RFC 7807 problem+json error shape), API-CI (1 — routes-vs-spec drift watchdog). Severity split 5H/8M/5L. Builds on Phase 14 (Sanctum scopes), Phase 18 (Sanctum/Gate parity for ability:integration:webhook), Phase 15 (LimitsPerPage trait), Phase 19 (POLICY-7 Gate::define('integration:webhook')). Tools: dedoc/scramble for auto-generated OpenAPI from Form Requests + JsonResource return types (Laravel-native, lower friction than l5-swagger annotations).

versioning_today.url_prefix: /api/v1/, /api/v2/ (v2 is a single-endpoint optimization for arrears report)

auth_today.creation: $user->createToken('device-name', $abilities) called from AuthController::login + AuthController::register — there is no UI for landlords to mint a PAT for integration use
auth_today.last_used_at: Sanctum tracks personal_access_tokens.last_used_at but no UI surfaces it
auth_today.audit_log: Phase-14 SecurityLog covers some auth events but token issue/revoke is NOT logged

rate_limit_today.headers_emitted: NONE in 200 responses — Laravel only emits Retry-After on 429 by default

webhook_today.inbound_only: Mpesa C2B + STK + B2C, IntaSend, KCB/Equity/Coop bank, WhatsApp inbound + status — all PROVIDER-PUSHED to PropManager, IP/signature validated
webhook_today.outbound_to_landlord: NONE — landlords cannot register their own integration webhook endpoints to receive payment/lease events
webhook_today.events_emitted_internally: Laravel events (PaymentReceived, InvoiceCreated, LeaseExpired, etc.) — wired to listeners + jobs internally, NOT exposed as webhook payloads

error_shape_today.no_problem_json: no RFC 7807 type/title/status/detail pattern; every endpoint shape may differ

qualityStandards.spec_authoritative: OpenAPI 3.1 spec must be GENERATED from code (FormRequest validation rules + JsonResource return types + route definitions). Manual hand-edits are forbidden — the spec is downstream of the code, not the other way around. Tooling: dedoc/scramble.
qualityStandards.versioning_url_prefix: Major versions are URL-prefixed (/api/v1/, /api/v2/...). Minor/patch evolution is backwards-compatible WITHIN a major. A new major requires a deprecation cycle before the prior is removed.
qualityStandards.deprecation_contract: When a route or response field is deprecated: (1) Sunset header MUST point to the removal date (RFC 8594 IMF-fixdate); (2) Deprecation header MUST be 'true' OR a date; (3) the API-deprecations changelog gets an entry; (4) minimum 6 months between Deprecation flag and removal. Operator-facing runbook: docs/runbooks/api-deprecation.md.
qualityStandards.ratelimit_headers: Every throttled response (200 OR 429) MUST emit X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset (epoch seconds). 429 bodies MUST be problem+json (type=https://propmanager.test/errors/rate-limit-exceeded, title, status, detail, retry_after_seconds).
qualityStandards.error_shape_authoritative: All /api/v1/* error responses MUST conform to RFC 7807 problem+json: { type (absolute URI), title, status, detail, instance (request path) }. Validation errors extend with { errors: { field: [...] } } to preserve client compatibility. Documented in docs/runbooks/api-deprecation.md alongside versioning.
qualityStandards.auth_self_serve: Landlord UI MUST allow: list tokens (name, scopes, created_at, last_used_at, last_used_ip), mint new (name + scope set), revoke (immediate). Token issue + revoke MUST write a SecurityLog entry. Revoked tokens MUST 401 within one request (not waiting for cache TTL).
qualityStandards.webhook_self_serve: Landlord UI MUST allow: register a webhook (URL + secret + event-type filter + active toggle), view delivery log (event, URL, status, response code, attempt #, last error), manually retry a failed delivery. Outbound deliveries MUST be HMAC-SHA256-signed (X-PropManager-Signature header), retry with exponential backoff (the existing Phase-16 RESIL pattern), and dead-letter after 5 failures.
qualityStandards.ci_drift_gate: Phase25CiTest::test_routes_match_spec asserts (a) every /api/* GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE route is documented in the generated OpenAPI spec, (b) every spec-documented endpoint resolves to a real route, (c) every Api controller method has a JsonResource return type OR an explicit response schema. Runs on every CI push; shrink-only (a baseline allow-list captures known partial-coverage endpoints — auth.* + webhooks.* — but new endpoints joining the allow-list requires a PR-described justification).

feedbackLoops.spec_generation_loop: php artisan scramble:export emits openapi.json on every CI build; the artifact is uploaded so reviewers can download + diff against the previous build. PR description gets an auto-comment if the spec diff is non-empty.
feedbackLoops.deprecation_email_loop: When a route gains a Deprecation header, the system reads its consumer list (personal_access_tokens that have hit that route in the last 30d) and queues an email — gives consumers minimum 6 months runway. dedoc/scramble's spec-diff feeds the trigger.
feedbackLoops.ratelimit_visibility_loop: X-RateLimit-* headers are added by a single middleware ApiRateLimitHeaders (registered in bootstrap/app.php api group); operators can grep their access logs for X-RateLimit-Remaining=0 to find consumers that are bouncing off the bucket.
feedbackLoops.webhook_delivery_loop: Failed webhook deliveries appear in the landlord UI's delivery log with a Retry button; retry uses the same job class as the original attempt + increments attempt count + writes another SecurityLog entry. After 5 attempts the delivery enters a dead-letter state (consistent with Phase-16 RESIL-8 WebhookDeadLetter pattern, but for OUTBOUND webhooks).
feedbackLoops.ci_routes_drift_loop: Phase25CiTest watchdog catches a developer who adds a new route but forgets the JsonResource OR who removes a route but leaves it documented. Failure message names the exact route + missing/extra side.

API-DOC.description: Machine-readable spec, served docs UI, generated changelog. Today's spec is the source code (routes/api.php + controllers + resources) — an integrator reads PHP to understand the API. This category installs dedoc/scramble (Laravel-native auto-generator), serves /api/v1/openapi.json + /docs interactive UI, and ratchets the spec quality via CI artifacts so spec drift is visible in PRs.

API-VERSION.description: Versioning policy + Sunset/Deprecation header enforcement + a public deprecations changelog. Today's two versions (v1, v2) coexist with no documented policy — when can a consumer expect v1 to die? With what notice? This category writes the policy, wires the headers, and publishes the consumer-facing deprecation log.

API-AUTH.description: API-key self-serve for landlords. Today only AuthController::login/register can mint a Sanctum PAT — there is no UI to create a PAT for an integration use case (e.g. landlord's QuickBooks sync). This category builds the management UI, tracks token last-used + IP, audit-logs issue/revoke, and documents the expiration/rotation policy.

API-RATELIMIT.description: Standardized rate-limit headers + 429 body shape. Today Laravel emits Retry-After on 429 but no envelope on 200 responses — consumers cannot pace themselves. This category adds X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset to every throttled endpoint and standardizes the 429 body to problem+json with retry_after_seconds.

API-WEBHOOK.description: Landlord-facing outbound webhooks. Today PropManager only RECEIVES webhooks (from Mpesa/IntaSend/banks/WhatsApp); landlords cannot register their own integration endpoint to receive payment/lease events. This category builds the subscription model + UI + outbound dispatch with HMAC signing + delivery log + retry + dead-letter + event catalog.

API-ERROR.description: Standardized RFC 7807 problem+json error shape across /api/v1/*. Today each error class has its own envelope (Laravel default ValidationException differs from Authentication differs from Authorization differs from RateLimit) — integrator code needs N parsers. This category unifies the shape and documents it in docs/runbooks/api-deprecation.md.

API-CI.description: CI watchdogs — routes vs spec drift. The cheap source-level assertions (the Phase-22/23/24 pattern) that prevent a contributor from adding a new route without a Resource, or removing a route while leaving it documented.

items.title: OpenAPI 3.1 spec as the source of truth — generated from code, served at /api/v1/openapi.json
items.evidence: The contract today is routes/api.php + 19 Api controllers + 9 Resources + 5 FormRequests — the integrator reads PHP source. No /api/v1/openapi.json endpoint exists. dedoc/scramble auto-generates the spec from FormRequest rules + JsonResource ->toArray() + route definitions + PHP return-type declarations, so PropManager gets the spec without hand-maintained annotations.
items.fix: (a) composer require dedoc/scramble; publish config; (b) ensure every Api controller method has a JsonResource return type (and where it returns response()->json directly, add an explicit response()->json(...)->headers + a docblock describing the shape — there are ~6 such sites in AuthController/HealthCheckController/MpesaController that the audit identifies); (c) /api/v1/openapi.json endpoint serves the generated spec (Scramble provides the route); (d) Phase25DocTest::test_openapi_endpoint_returns_valid_3_1_spec (HTTP 200, content-type application/openapi+json, OpenAPI 3.1.x version, all 57 v1 routes present in paths).

items.title: Interactive docs UI served at /docs (Swagger UI or Redoc)
items.evidence: Even with the spec served, integrators want a clickable + try-it-out UI. Scramble ships with a Swagger UI variant out of the box. Auth question: today's API consumers are landlord/super-admin operators who already have Inertia auth, so gating /docs behind the existing web session is the right friction level (a publicly browsable docs UI is a Phase-27 BI/marketing decision, not Phase 25).
items.fix: (a) enable Scramble's UI; (b) mount /docs in routes/web.php under auth + role:landlord|super_admin middleware; (c) Phase25DocTest::test_docs_endpoint_is_authenticated (HTTP 302 to /login when unauthenticated, HTTP 200 for an authenticated landlord, HTTP 403 for an authenticated tenant).

items.title: API changelog generated from spec diff in CI
items.evidence: When v1 evolves, consumers want a changelog they can read. The spec diff between CI runs is the source of truth — a tool like openapi-diff produces a human-readable breaking-vs-non-breaking summary. The CI step writes the diff to PR comments AND appends to docs/api/CHANGELOG.md on main.
items.fix: (a) new workflow that compares the generated spec against the merge-base spec; (b) breaking changes (removed paths/parameters, narrowed types) fail the build unless the PR title contains '[api-breaking]' (forcing explicit acknowledgement); (c) the on-main job appends a CHANGELOG entry. Phase25CiTest::test_api_changelog_format_is_valid (parses CHANGELOG.md, every entry has version + date + change-type).

items.title: API versioning policy + 6-month deprecation contract documented
items.evidence: v1 and v2 coexist (v2 is the single arrears optimization). No policy doc says when v1 dies, what notice consumers get, what the upgrade path looks like. An integrator pulling from /api/v1/* TODAY has no contractual confidence the URL won't 410 next quarter.
items.fix: (a) Write docs/runbooks/api-deprecation.md covering: URL-prefix major versioning, the Sunset+Deprecation header contract (RFC 8594), the 6-month minimum notice contract, the upgrade-path-must-be-documented rule, the parallel-version-support rule (v1 keeps running until Sunset date), and the consumer-notification process; (b) Phase25DocTest::test_api_deprecation_runbook_covers_the_contract (file exists + key sections present).

items.title: Sunset + Deprecation headers wired via middleware
items.evidence: When a route is deprecated, the contract says Sunset + Deprecation headers MUST flow on every response — but Laravel has no built-in middleware for this. A new middleware reads a per-route attribute (e.g. via Route::middleware('deprecated:2026-12-01')) and emits both headers.
items.fix: (a) Build ApiVersionHeaders middleware that reads route()->getAction('deprecated_at') and emits Sunset: <RFC1123 date> + Deprecation: true; (b) register on /api/v1/* routes; (c) Phase25VersionTest::test_deprecation_middleware_emits_both_headers (a route marked deprecated_at returns both headers in its 200 response, an undeprecated route does not).

items.title: Consumer-facing API-deprecations changelog (docs/api/deprecations.md)
items.evidence: Operator-facing api-deprecation.md (VERSION-1) describes the policy; consumers need a separate consumer-facing changelog that lists every active deprecation with date-flagged + sunset-date + upgrade-path link. Today: empty (no deprecations) but the file exists with the schema documented + a baseline entry pointing at the v1→v2 arrears endpoint pattern.
items.fix: (a) New docs/api/deprecations.md with a documented entry format (route, deprecated_at, sunset_at, replacement, upgrade_doc_url); (b) the API-VERSION-2 middleware writes an entry to this file via a make:command artisan invocation when a new deprecated_at is added; (c) Phase25DocTest::test_deprecations_log_is_parseable (each entry has the required fields).

items.title: API key management UI for landlords — list / mint / revoke
items.evidence: Today Sanctum PATs are minted only by AuthController::login (mobile-app flow with device_name). A landlord who wants to connect QuickBooks/Zapier has NO way to issue a token from the UI. Sanctum supports it natively ($user->createToken('label', $abilities)); the gap is the Vue UI + controller.
items.fix: (a) ApiTokenController with index/store/destroy; store accepts name (1-50 chars) + scopes (subset of {landlord:manage, integration:webhook}); response includes plaintext token ONCE (Sanctum pattern) with a copy-button + UI warning that it cannot be retrieved again; (b) index lists current tokens with last_used_at + last_used_ip + scopes (NEVER the plaintext); (c) destroy revokes immediately and 401s subsequent requests within one tick; (d) Phase25AuthTest::test_landlord_can_mint_and_revoke_personal_access_token + test_revoked_token_401s_immediately + test_tenant_role_cannot_mint_token + test_token_plaintext_is_never_persisted_after_creation.

items.title: Token last-used-at + last-used-ip surfaced in UI
items.evidence: Sanctum tracks personal_access_tokens.last_used_at automatically (its TokenAuth middleware updates it on every request). It does NOT track the IP that last used the token — but that's the security signal a landlord needs to spot a leaked token (token last used from an unfamiliar IP = compromise indicator).
items.fix: (a) Add last_used_ip varchar(45) column to personal_access_tokens (nullable); (b) New TrackTokenLastUsedIp middleware writes $request->ip() to the active token's last_used_ip on each authenticated /api/* request (uses Sanctum's currentAccessToken()); (c) UI displays both columns. Phase25AuthTest::test_authenticated_api_call_updates_last_used_ip.

items.title: Token issue + revoke writes SecurityLog audit entries
items.evidence: Phase-14 SecurityLog covers login/logout/password-reset but not token lifecycle. A landlord investigating 'who else is calling our API' needs an audit trail.
items.fix: (a) store() writes SecurityLog(event_type='api_token_issued', user_id, metadata={token_id, name, scopes}); (b) destroy() writes SecurityLog(event_type='api_token_revoked', user_id, metadata={token_id, name, scopes}); (c) Phase25AuthTest::test_token_issue_and_revoke_write_security_log.

items.title: Token expiration policy + UI rotation reminder
items.evidence: Sanctum supports per-token expiration but PropManager's PATs today never expire. NIST + OWASP API Security Top 10 say long-lived tokens accumulate risk — a 12-month default expiration with UI reminder at 60d before expiry is the conservative posture.
items.fix: (a) Set config('sanctum.expiration') = 60 * 24 * 365 (12 months); (b) ApiTokens/Index displays a banner when any active token's expires_at is within 60 days; (c) Phase25AuthTest::test_token_default_expiration_is_12_months.

items.title: X-RateLimit-Limit / X-RateLimit-Remaining / X-RateLimit-Reset on every throttled response
items.evidence: Laravel's ThrottleRequests emits Retry-After on 429 but no headers on the successful 200 — consumers cannot pace themselves. Industry convention (GitHub, Stripe, Twilio) is X-RateLimit-Limit (bucket size), X-RateLimit-Remaining (remaining in window), X-RateLimit-Reset (epoch seconds when bucket refills). Laravel's RateLimiter::limiter() exposes the metadata; the middleware just adds the headers.
items.fix: (a) Build ApiRateLimitHeaders middleware that reads RateLimiter::limiter($bucket)->attempts() + limit + decay and writes X-RateLimit-* on the response; (b) register globally on the /api/* group AFTER ThrottleRequests so 429s also get the envelope; (c) Phase25RateLimitTest::test_api_response_carries_ratelimit_headers + test_429_response_carries_full_envelope.

items.title: 429 response body conforms to RFC 7807 problem+json with retry_after_seconds
items.evidence: Laravel's default 429 body is { 'message': 'Too Many Attempts.' } — adequate but not standardised. RFC 7807 problem+json is the API-design industry consensus (Stripe + GitHub + Slack all follow it).
items.fix: (a) Exception handler intercepts ThrottleRequestsException and returns response()->json([type, title, status, detail, retry_after_seconds, instance], 429, [Content-Type: application/problem+json]); (b) Phase25RateLimitTest::test_429_body_is_problem_json + test_retry_after_seconds_present_and_matches_header.

items.title: Per-token rate-limit override policy (operator can raise the bucket for a trusted integrator)
items.evidence: Default throttle:api = 60/min works for individual operators but a high-volume integrator (QuickBooks Sync polling 200 tenants every 5min) needs a raised bucket. Industry pattern: per-token multiplier (1x, 5x, 10x).
items.fix: (a) Add rate_limit_multiplier int column (default 1) to personal_access_tokens; (b) Middleware reads the active token's multiplier and adjusts the resolved bucket size; (c) UI displays multiplier (read-only for landlord; editable by super-admin); (d) Phase25RateLimitTest::test_token_with_5x_multiplier_gets_5x_bucket.

items.title: Webhook subscription model + UI — landlords register their own integration endpoints
files: app/Jobs/DeliverWebhookJob.php (new — outbound dispatch with HMAC + retry)
items.evidence: PropManager today only INGESTS webhooks (Mpesa/IntaSend/banks/WhatsApp). Landlords integrating with QuickBooks/Zapier/their own ERP need to RECEIVE payment.created, invoice.created, lease.signed events. This category builds the subscription model (URL + secret + event filter + active flag), the outbound dispatcher with HMAC-SHA256 signing (X-PropManager-Signature header), and exponential backoff (Phase-16 RESIL pattern).
items.fix: (a) Migration creates webhook_subscriptions (id, landlord_id FK, url, secret, events JSON, active bool, created_at); (b) UI list/create/edit/delete + 'Test delivery' button that POSTs a sample payload; (c) DeliverWebhookJob queued on payments queue (Phase-16 priority); (d) HMAC signature middleware on outbound; (e) Phase25WebhookTest::test_landlord_can_register_a_webhook + test_outbound_payload_is_hmac_signed + test_inactive_subscription_does_not_receive_events + test_event_filter_respects_subscription.

items.title: Webhook delivery log + retry-from-UI
items.evidence: Without a delivery log the landlord cannot diagnose a flaky integrator endpoint. The log records every attempt (request, response, status, timing), supports manual retry from the UI, and dead-letters after 5 failed attempts (consistent with Phase-16 inbound WebhookDeadLetter).
items.fix: (a) Migration creates webhook_deliveries (id, subscription_id FK, event_type, payload JSON, attempt int, http_status, response_body, dispatched_at, completed_at, error nullable); (b) DeliverWebhookJob writes a row per attempt; (c) UI Show page lists deliveries with filter + retry button; (d) Phase25WebhookTest::test_failed_delivery_is_retryable + test_dead_letter_after_5_attempts.

items.title: Event catalog — list of available webhook event types + payload schemas
items.evidence: An integrator registering a webhook needs to know what event types exist + what payload shape to expect. Without a catalog the landlord guesses. A registry + docs file + UI dropdown (sourced from the registry) makes it self-serve.
items.fix: (a) WebhookEventRegistry exposes available event types (payment.received, payment.refunded, invoice.created, invoice.paid, lease.signed, lease.expired, tenant.invited, etc.); (b) docs/api/webhook-events.md documents each event's payload shape (JSON example + field meanings); (c) WebhookSubscriptionController's create UI dropdown is sourced from the registry; (d) Phase25WebhookTest::test_event_catalog_lists_all_registered_events + test_payload_shape_matches_documented_schema.

items.title: Standardized RFC 7807 problem+json error shape across /api/v1/*
files: app/Exceptions/ApiProblemRenderer.php (new — converts an exception to a problem+json response)
items.evidence: Today validation 422 + auth 401 + authz 403 + ratelimit 429 + 500 all have different envelopes. An integrator-side error parser needs N branches. RFC 7807 problem+json unifies the shape: { type (absolute URI), title, status, detail, instance, extensions...}.
items.fix: (a) ApiProblemRenderer maps Laravel exceptions (ValidationException, AuthenticationException, AuthorizationException, ThrottleRequestsException, ModelNotFoundException, generic Throwable) to problem+json; (b) Handler::render() detects api/* paths and routes through the renderer; (c) ValidationException retains its 'errors' field for client backwards compat but adds type/title/status/instance; (d) Phase25ErrorTest::test_all_api_error_shapes_are_problem_json (drive each error class through and assert shape).

items.title: CI watchdog — routes vs OpenAPI spec drift
items.evidence: A future contributor adds /api/v1/landlord/widgets without a JsonResource — Scramble can't document the response shape. Or removes /api/v1/landlord/buildings/{id}/units while leaving it in the spec — consumers 404. The watchdog catches both.
items.fix: (a) Phase25CiTest::test_every_api_route_is_in_the_spec walks $router->getRoutes() filtered by /api/* and asserts each appears in the generated openapi.json paths; (b) test_every_spec_endpoint_resolves_to_a_real_route walks paths and asserts each resolves; (c) test_every_api_controller_method_returns_a_resource asserts every Api controller public method has a JsonResource or JsonResponse return type; (d) shrink-only baseline allows known-partial endpoints (auth/* + webhooks/* + health/* + metrics) via an explicit allow-list.
